> **Plane MCP pre-flight (Copilot ‚Äî check this before the Plane path steps below):**
>
> Scan your available tool list for at least one tool matching `mcp__plane__*`, `plane_*`, or `mcp_plane_*`.
> - **None found ‚Üí STOP.** Print: "Error: Plane MCP tools are not accessible. In VS Code, open the MCP panel (View ‚Üí MCP Servers), ensure the `plane` server shows as Connected, and retry. Do not fall back to creating markdown tickets." Do not continue to the Markdown path.
> - **Found ‚Üí note the active prefix** (e.g. `mcp__plane__` or `plane_`) and use it for every tool call in this session.
> - **If some `plane_*` tools are found but write tools like `create_work_item` are absent**, the tool list was truncated due to context budget overflow ‚Äî re-run `install.sh` to enable the tool-filter proxy, then restart VS Code.
>
> **If `.claude/plane-config.md` exists, the Markdown path is off-limits** regardless of MCP status ‚Äî never silently degrade to local TKT-*.md files when the project uses Plane.


# Promote Stub Tickets

Flip tickets from "brainstormed stub" to "committed work" ‚Äî the human gate between `/brainstorm` output and `/ticket-investigate`.

## Pre-flight: detect backend

- If `.claude/plane-config.md` exists ‚Üí **Plane path**.
- Else if `.claude/ticket-config.md` + `tickets/` ‚Üí **Markdown path**.
- Else: error.

## Input (both backends)

- `--all` ‚Äî promote every stub in the project.
- Otherwise: one or more ticket IDs (bare numbers resolve per project identifier / prefix + padding).


## Plane path

### Pre-flight

1. Load `.claude/plane-config.md`.
2. Resolve the set:
   - **`--all`**: URL handoff. Read `.claude/plane-config.md`'s `## View URLs` section and print the `Stubs` URL as a clickable markdown link:
     ```
     Open: [Stub tickets in {IDENT}]({url})

     Multi-select the ones you want to promote, then bulk-remove the `stub` label in the Plane UI.
     (If a selected ticket has a `plan-ticket` parent that's also a stub, remember to promote the parent too.)
     ```
     Then stop. No MCP fetch.

     **If `## View URLs` is absent** (pre-view-URLs install): compose the Stubs URL inline ‚Äî don't go spec-spelunking. Template: `{PLANE_BASE_URL}/{workspace-slug}/projects/{project-uuid}/issues/?labels={stub-label-uuid}&order_by=-priority`. Source for each piece:
     - `workspace-slug` ‚Üí `- Workspace slug:` line of `plane-config.md`. If that value is the literal placeholder `(inferred from Plane MCP server env vars; no need to hard-code)`, fall back to `~/.claude.json` ‚Üí `mcpServers.plane.env.PLANE_WORKSPACE_SLUG`; final fallback `AskUserQuestion("workspace slug? e.g. throughline")`. Write the resolved value back into `plane-config.md`'s header so it's cached.
     - `PLANE_BASE_URL` ‚Üí `~/.claude.json` ‚Üí `mcpServers.plane.env.PLANE_BASE_URL`; fallback `AskUserQuestion`.
     - `project-uuid` ‚Üí from `- Project ID:` line of `plane-config.md`.
     - `stub-label-uuid` ‚Üí from `- stub:` line under `## Label IDs (workspace-standard)` in `plane-config.md`.

     Append the `## View URLs` section to `plane-config.md` (at least the Stubs URL). Then print the link and stop.
   - **Explicit IDs**: `mcp__plane__retrieve_work_item_by_identifier` for each ‚Äî proceed with the per-ticket steps below.

### Steps (explicit-ID path only)

For each ticket in the set:

1. Verify it has the `stub` label and is in `Backlog` state. If not, skip with a notice (not an error).
2. **Remove the `stub` label** via `mcp__plane__update_work_item(labels=<existing minus stub>)`. Fetch current labels first; strip the stub ID; write back.
3. If the ticket has a parent work item (its "plan ticket"), verify the parent exists and is Backlog or Ready. If the parent also has the `stub` label (an unpromoted plan ticket), promote the parent recursively ‚Äî same remove-stub-label step. A plan ticket is promoted at most once per call.

Plane has no `stub/` folder to move out of (labels do that work). State stays `Backlog` ‚Äî promotion is a **label-removal only**, not a state transition.

### Output (explicit-ID path)

```
Promoted {N} tickets{, M plan tickets}:
  Tickets:
    {ID}  "{title}"
    ...
  Plan tickets (auto-promoted):
    {ID}  "{title}"

Skipped {K}: {reasons}

Next: /ticket-investigate {first ID}  (investigate them in turn)
```

### Rules (Plane path)

- `--all` hands off to Plane's UI ‚Äî don't fetch stubs in the agent. The UI's multi-select + bulk-label-remove is faster than an agent loop.
- Explicit-ID path: only operate on work items with the `stub` label. Skip others quietly.
- Do NOT change state ‚Äî leave them in `Backlog` (they were already there).
- Plan-ticket parents are auto-promoted (in the explicit-ID path) to keep the `/ti` parent-read logic consistent.


## Markdown path

*(Unchanged. See `archive-commands/ticket-promote.md`.)*

### Input (markdown)

Parse flags first:
- `--all` ‚Äî promote every `TKT-*.md` stub AND every `EPIC-*.md` file in `{tickets-dir}/stub/`.

Remaining arguments are ticket IDs. Resolution rules:
- **Fully-qualified IDs** (e.g., `TKT-102a`) match exactly: `TKT-102a.md`.
- **Bare numbers** (e.g., `102`) resolve as a prefix match: include the canonical `TKT-<padded>.md` (using the prefix and zero-padding from `.claude/ticket-config.md`) plus any letter-suffix variants (`TKT-102a.md`, `TKT-102b.md`, ...). Do NOT match other numeric variants like `TKT-1020.md`.

If `--all` is given, explicit IDs are ignored. Zero IDs and no `--all` ‚Üí error.

### Pre-flight

- `.claude/ticket-config.md` must exist. If not, tell the user to run `/ticket-install` and stop.
- `{tickets-dir}/stub/` must exist. If not, report that there are no stubs to promote and stop.

### Steps

1. Resolve the set of tickets to promote (expand each arg to matching files in `{tickets-dir}/stub/`).
2. Collect referenced epics ‚Äî for each matched ticket, read its `epic:` frontmatter and gather unique epic slugs. Each epic file is `{tickets-dir}/stub/EPIC-<slug>.md`. If `--all`, include every `EPIC-*.md` in stub regardless of references.
3. Promote each file (tickets first, then epics):
   - Verify it's in `stub/` and has `status: stub`.
   - `mv {tickets-dir}/stub/<name>.md {tickets-dir}/<name>.md`.
   - Update frontmatter: `status: stub` ‚Üí `status: open`; set `updated: <today>`.
   - Each epic promoted at most once per call.

### Rules (markdown path)

- Only promote files whose frontmatter reads `status: stub`. Skip others with a notice.
- Do NOT touch files in the active set or terminal subfolders.
- Do NOT investigate; promotion is a status flip + file move only.
- Epics referenced by promoted tickets are auto-promoted so `/ti`'s same-directory epic lookup works.

## Compatibility Notes

- **MCP tool mapping:** In VS Code Copilot, Plane MCP tools are typically exposed as `plane_<tool_name>` (e.g. `plane_create_work_item`), not `mcp__plane__<tool_name>` (Claude Code convention). The Plane MCP pre-flight above detects which prefix is active and uses it for the session. For explicit IDs, prefer direct identifier lookup; if unavailable, use bounded project-scoped lookup + exact match.
- **AskUserQuestion:** Copilot has no equivalent structured-question tool. When the Claude source says "use AskUserQuestion", ask the user in chat with a numbered or bulleted choice and wait for a reply.
- **Parallel subagents ‚Üí sequential loop:** if the Claude source fans out subagents in parallel (e.g. ticket-investigate multi-ticket, ticket-batch), run them as a sequential loop in one agent session instead. Output and behavior are identical; only wall-clock differs.
- **Backend dispatch:** the "Pre-flight: detect backend" block is authoritative in both hosts. `.claude/plane-config.md` presence routes to the Plane path; otherwise the Markdown path runs.
